item.title: BEST FRIENd
item.content:encoded: Personally I'd want to shoot the shit with Tayn't like just chill and watch his battling beatles and smoke with him if i think of hanging out with him is that an option? also the gun with a vagina part killlled me. I'd like to imagine dado meeting him

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: i just loved this!!! very fun character this was interesting. 1+

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So… A possibly stoned Eldritch Abomination is sitting in his own pocket dimension, possibly summoning people that think real hard about getting high or having some hocus pocus in effect to do it for him… And he sells them the best weed in the entire universe for basically at a point, free…

Well damn, Eldritch Abominations seem pretty chill.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Wait wait.

A friend pointed out that plant seeds require exposure to air in order to germinate.

So the actual worst-case scenario would be for a user to inhale a seed. It wouldn't have fertilizer (at first) but it would have moisture and air.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thank you! :)

Feel free to use any of that. I'm marking the previous post CC0.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is fucked up.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So what happens if you chew it?

I'm guessing it's like chewing a plastic grocery bag, only stronger. Your teeth can't tear it. Saliva can't dissolve it. If you swallowed it, it would just take up space in your stomach forever, or pass right through, depending on whether it got stuck or not. Stomach acid wouldn't do anything to it. But your body wouldn't have any particular immune response to it either, since it can't be interacted with by destructive chemical processes, even at the microscopic or atomic levels.

(Guess you can't really cut it up, either. Guess Tayn't takes care of that long before the sale and what you get is what you get.)

But here's the part that'll really bake your noodle: It's possible if THC on the surface of the sample came into contact with cannabinoid receptors somewhere in the digestive tract, and became lodged there, it would occupy the receptor site indefinitely.

You would become permanently high off of this [REDACTED].

Unless some physical trauma ripped the plant away from the cells like velcro, that is. Since the receptor cites are at the molecular scale, that's pretty likely. But if it got stuck just right… say, up against the intenstinal glands of an impacted colon…

Oh, but then it's an invincible plant surrounded by feces.

Watch out for them seeds, bro. >:)

Edit: Meant to make this a new post, not a reply. Sorry.

I hereby release the contents of this post into the public domain under CC0. Go nuts, kinch. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So… it's an Ayy Lmao?
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Creepy, hilarious, and evocative. I love it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Actually, I think the message to get from this is don't /steal/ drugs, because this sounds like some excellent, excellent ████.

item.title: Re: Question for the Tag Committee
item.content:encoded: I agree with this reasoning. I was just unsure. Thanks!

item.title: Re: Question for the Tag Committee
item.content:encoded: I feel like because the temperature change or heat proliferation is occurring within the pocket dimension and not in our own, the tag doesn't really apply. If you want to add it still, I'd go with thermodynamic. Thermal is more for something like an artifact that implodes forever if it's containment is above a certain temperature, or if a contagion spreads its disease more while it's colder.

item.title: Re: Question for the Tag Committee
item.content:encoded: No Tayn't is literally able to turn up that HEAT.

item.title: Re: Question for the Tag Committee
item.content:encoded: Is that what's happening? I just figured he was doing some epic hotboxing.

item.title: Question for the Tag Committee
item.content:encoded: Would it be justified to give this article a "thermal" or "thermodynamic" tag due to the scip's ability to, like, make the temperature raise in its little dimension house?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It strikes me as really unnatural that someone would go on for two paragraphs like that

I do like conservation of detail, and I sometimes am forced to point out unnaturally long-winded segments of dialogue. Usually in these cases, it's due to the author committing expo-sin-tion, and bloating the dialogue with story. Here, the dialogue does indeed go on for while, but it's overall pretty competent in masking the story, and it does feel like an actual recounting of events from an actual person as opposed to a story-touting mouthpiece… sure, there's a couple parts that do go on, but it makes sense for the interviewer to allow them to say their piece without too much interjection - he's telling them just about everything they need to know. In such a setting, I imagine you'd want to give the guy breathing room to fill in the blanks.

There's a couple places that could stand to have some superfluous details shaved off, definitely, but it doesn't strike me as unnatural.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fair enough, then I'll just chalk it up that I find long, multi-paragraph dialogue unpleasant to read, and I believe that the same amount of information can be related in less bulk.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm…. conflicted about this, but I think I'll probably upvote. When the concept is discussed in plain clinical tone, it sounds terrible, and the "it BRUTALLY KILLS anyone who steals from it" bit sounded like the kind of graphic violence people throw into coldposts to make them spooky. (I know that isn't what's happening, but it reads that way.). That, and the interview has some show-don't-tell problems in there. (For example: "He just looked like something weird trying to be human.")

Edit: Also, you've got a "do illegal drugs" in the containment, which is colloquial.

All of that said, the interview and especially the log were fantastic, and pulled me right back in. Bug time indeed.

Edit edit: If I were critiquing this on the forum, I would probably recommend cutting back on the description to get to the logs faster.

(Also, per the GAW inversion, first off lol, and second, you guys know that GAW aren't actually against weed, right? I assume gaming isn't an actual requirement either. Not that Tayn't could figure out IRC.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Most chemical testing of biological materials is destructive. How did they analyze genetics or THC content when the stuff is indestructible excluding when it's in a blunt wrap?

Once it's been burned by the user, presumably the smoke and ashes could be collected and analyzed. It wouldn't be the ideal method, but remember that the whole point of smoking is to vaporize the chemicals inside of the plant matter while leaving them semi-intact, and they're not lacking in samples.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Love the character here, and I hope to see more from them in the future.

So is he … a weed dealer against games?

I could actually see this being the result of a previous exchange with one of the GAW members. Perhaps one of them stole from him, and successfully evaded retribution due to anomalous hijinks
(alternatively, it's simply aware of their name, and is offended. 'what did I ever do, my man?'). Now Tayn't just happens to have a tainted perspective on games in general due to vaguely understanding humans to begin with.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A conversation is different from an interview or an interrogation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Generally, a conversation doesn't have someone going on for several full sentences several times without most people perceiving it as pushy and rude. Also, it sucks to read for me(admittedly I generally hate long dialogue).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Stuff I don't like:

  • Most chemical testing of biological materials is destructive. How did they analyze genetics or THC content when the stuff is indestructible excluding when it's in a blunt wrap?
  • The Foundation just sent a D-Class in there to die. Shit's fucked up man.

Stuff I like:

  • It's somewhere between an old folk tale and a modern dope-pusher/gangbanger, with a hint of Lovecraft and a wee hint of something Faustian going on.
  • Overall the characterization of the dude/demon/fae/ogre/gangster is really good. I could see its dialogue coming from a Lovecraftian horror, a Medieval demon, or just someone who is SUPER stoned. Hitting that balance couldn't have been easy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So much of me to give.

No. Weed against Gamers.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
He said he hated video games.

Stoners against vidya new GOI confirmed

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This reminded me of Manly Wade Wellman. The image of a being from Somewhat Else trying to deal with smooth apes from here, sometime getting it right, other times less so - very appealing. Especially that we never find just what It thinks is going on, or why.

Reminds me, my pipe needs refilling.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
<camera pans down to see D-3459, who had been killed by SCP-2542>

This is your brain on Keter. Stay Thaumiel, kids.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Excellent excellent imagery - I'm a sucker for things attempting to be human through distorted perceptions. Which is why it was somewhat off-putting when I got to the exploration logs and Tayn't (is that a taint joke?) just… talked like a drug dealer? IDK, that's very much my bias, I think. Still upvoting, obviously.

Keep making good shit, kinch.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: …motherfucker.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think we just have to agree to disagree on this one.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So is he … a weed dealer against games?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I actually think the "before" feels more like realistic dialogue than the "after". When people talk, they tend to go off on side tangents, repeat themselves, and use a lot of filler.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
On one hand, I really like the image. I seriously love that fucking image. Holy shit the final log was amazing. I'd hug that weed baron forever and he's the cutest, cuddliest little bugaroo.

My downvote is for the interview. It strikes me as really unnatural that someone would go on for two paragraphs like that, and much of the interviewee's discription was difficult for me to follow once it went on too long. I found myself constantly wanting to skip the interview, stop reading, and go downvote the thing immediately. One particularly long section is okay, but when its every response it can get very difficult to just hear a monologue. A lot of what is said there can be wittled down to its most significant parts. Compare a section here:

+ show block
– hide block

I: Yet you say he was behind these murders?

DS I told my friend about him. My friend Johnny. I needed someone to hold onto this weed. I needed someone to help me deal. I told him how to meet Tayn't, and he did. He was a great partner. Knew him since grade school, you know. But he always got greedy. Always mouthy. Couldn't hold a secret either. Stupid idea.

I: Why would Tayn't harm your friend?

DS: Johnny'd been stealing from him for a while. Told some friends like him about the magic elf we got weed from. They'd all been stealing from him for a week before Tayn't found out.


to
+ show block
– hide block

I: Yet you say he was behind these murders?

DS: Started with my friend, Johnny. Needed someone to help me deal. Knew him since grade school, you know. But he always got greedy. Couldn't hold a secret either.

I: Why would Tayn't harm your friend?

DS: Johnny'd been stealing from him for a while. Told some friends. They'd all been stealing for a week before they got found out.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This weird plant bug elf alien demon fairy dude just wants to have friends, but he's so different from humans that all he can really do is try to buy it with his weird alien weed. I feel bad for him. While it's grossly disproportionate what he does to people who steal from him, but like, you'd have to be such a huge dick to do that in the first place, so it operates like one of those old fables where things are good if you're honest but if you're greedy you get punished real bad.

Solid +1, and long live Tayn't, the Weed Baron.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Just don't do drugs. J-just… don't do them. This has been a PSA. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: gross in a good way. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Though the mills of God grind slowly; Yet they grind exceeding small;
Though with patience He stands waiting, With exactness grinds He all."

Thanks to scans, scarhaver, decibelles, tygently, and aideneldritch for giving this a look-see. Here's an SCP about a friendly magic drug dealer who gets betrayed.
